{{Quote|There was a coup attempt? We ''never'' hear anything on Terephon.|[[Entora Zar]]|<ref name="tempest" />}}
'''Terephon''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]], located in the [[Transitory Mists]]. The Mists, which blocked all long-range and [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] transmissions, ensured Terephon's isolation and helped shape its reputation as a backwater in the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Consortium]]. Nevertheless, rising from the marshy plains of the planet were [[Hapan]] cities and mountain ranges; the world became popular for hunting, and wealthy nobles often built retreats on Terephon.

The planet offered little to the Hapes Cluster other than several of the amazon guards who protected the [[Hapan Royal House|Hapan Royal Family]]. However, once [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] began to interact with one another, Terephon became the occasional location of galactic intrigue. As with many Hapan worlds, it was presided over by a [[Ducha]]. Terephon was generally not a notable planet in the galaxy, though during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], it was embroiled in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide conflict that reached Hapes. Later that year, the planet the site of a [[Duel on Terephon|climactic duel]] where [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady]] of the [[Lumiya's Sith|Sith]] [[Lumiya]].

==Description==
Terephon was a world of contrasts.<ref name="tempest" /><ref name="sacrifice" /> There were areas of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that were covered in hundreds if not thousands of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] of bogs and marshlands; other areas were instead great expansive moors corrugated with low hills. Much of these areas were inaccessible by land, and [[Starship/Legends|starships]] would later be used to move over the moors. The moorlands acted as the favorite retreats of [[Politics/Legends|politicians]] and the wealthy, and [[murg]]s, reptilian creatures native to Terephon that dwelt underground, were often tamed and used in hunting, a major past-time for the rich. There were areas from which cliffs arose, with cave warrens running through them,<ref name="tempest" /> and the moorlands eventually gave way to mountains and mesas. Opulent [[Hapan]] cities were constructed on the drier parts of the planet, with lush gardens among their towering spires.<ref name="sacrifice" />

==History==
===Hapan backwater===
Terephon became known as the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of many of the amazon guards who made up the [[Queen Mother]]'s royal protectors, one of the most notable of these being [[Astarta]], a close friend of [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] and his personal bodyguard.<ref name="copl" /> The planet was presided over by a [[Ducha]], who looked over Terephon as a personal holding. Due to its location in the [[Transitory Mists]], however, Terephon became a world secluded from the rest of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], and was seen as an isolated planet, as long-range and [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] transmissions could not reach it.<ref name="tempest" /> The Mists also acted as a haven for [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and bandits, making flight around Terephon a risky business.<ref name="sacrifice" />

===Yuuzhan Vong War===
{{Quote|I can assure you that Terephon will vote to render aid.|[[Lol Miilarta]]|<ref name="aoc2" />}}
During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] Terephon, represented in the Hapes Consortium by [[Lol Miilarta]], pledged its support to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] when Ambassador [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] arrived to solicit aid in the struggle with the powerful [[Yuuzhan Vong|invaders]].<ref name="aoc2">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref>

===Second Galactic Civil War===
In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] Terephon continued to remain a relative backwater in terms of the Hapes Cluster; the planetary militia still used outdated [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. Yet the conflict rapidly developing between [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] came to [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], and would see Terephon caught up in its machinations. Queen Mother [[Tenel Ka Djo]] had pledged her support, along with three battle fleets, to the Galactic Alliance. Many Hapan nobles joined together in their deep-rooted dislike of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and this growing lack of Hapan independence to form the [[Heritage Council]], of whom [[Galney (Ducha of Terephon)|Ducha Galney]] of Terephon, seen as one of Tenel Ka's most loyal supporters, was a member. Thus when [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Zekk]] arrived at Terephon and came to realize that the Ducha was gathering her fleet long before she could have known about the plot to assassinate the Queen, Galney sent a squadron of [[Miy'til starfighter|Miy'tils]] to bomb her own hunting retreat, [[Villa Solis]], and kill the Jedi. The attempt failed, and Galney was later defeated.<ref name="tempest" /> Weeks later, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] cruised the Transitory Mists above Terephon following a call from [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] that the woman he hunted, [[Lumiya]], was to be found there. They engaged in a dogfight over Terephon's surface before the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] was forced down to land on a mesa overlooking a Terephonian city. There, Skywalker defeated and killed Lumiya in a [[Duel on Terephon|duel]].<ref name="sacrifice" />

==Inhabitants==
The Hapans who inhabited the planet were known as Terephonians, the majority of whom dwelt in the planet's classic Hapan cities.<ref name="sacrifice" /> Hapans differed from baseline [[Human/Legends|Human]] stock in a number of ways, most notably in that they possessed poor night vision and were considered to be, on average, more attractive than was the typical Human standard for beauty. Those who lived on Terephon, especially out in the moors, were accustomed to hear little of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] or of the Consortium's affairs, and often regarded the world they lived on as a backwater.<ref name="tempest" />

==Notable locations==
===Villa Solis===
{{Main|Villa Solis}}
Ducha Galney of Terephon lived at times at the [[Villa Solis]], remotely located in the Terephonian moorlands. It was constructed as her private hunting retreat, and was only accessible by air. The architecture was simple, and the villa was made up of a number of squat, domed round buildings, built from white [[gratenite]]. It had relatively high security, and a secret hangar built into the gratenite cliffs which rose up at the Villa's rear. The Ducha destroyed her villa in 40 ABY in an attempt to kill Jedi Knights Jaina Solo and Zekk, who had discovered evidence of Galney's [[Treason/Legends|treason]].<ref name="tempest" />

==Behind the scenes==
Terephon was, until ''Tempest'', a generic, unseen Hapan planet in the Consortium, notable as the homeworld of the amazon guards.<ref name="copl" /> It was mentioned again in [[James Luceno]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' as one of the worlds which favored granting the New Republic aid,<ref name="aoc2" /> and was visited by the protagonists of ''Tempest'', the novel in which it was first seen and described.<ref name="tempest" /> It later served as the location of the duel between Luke Skywalker and Lumiya in ''Sacrifice'', where on one occasion it was incorrectly spelled 'Tepheron'.<ref name="sacrifice" />
